steps.title: Identify what the blank must mean
steps.content: The phrase "To improve this system" tells us the blank names whatever was proposed to fix the flawed pay-upon-receipt postal system. The example given — a postage stamp shifting payment to the sender — is a systematic change intended to correct a problem. The blank needs a word meaning changes made to correct or improve a flawed system.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'investigations': Means attempts to understand or examine something. Affixing stamps is not an investigation; it is a corrective action. ✗
  • Choice B — 'expansions': Means additions that increase size or scope. The text describes changes to existing policy, not growth in the number of policies. ✗
  • Choice C — 'reforms': Means systematic changes made to correct a problem — exactly what "to improve this system" signals and what the postage stamp example illustrates. ✓
  • Choice D — 'possessions': Means items of personal property. Nothing in the context frames the proposed changes as possessions. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
